content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                          Date : 6/16/76


                    Natalie West                    M-76-502
From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

Subject: Letter from Minot W. Tripp, Contra Costa Democratic
         Party, dated May 21, 1976


     During the week of May 31, I spoke with Minot
Tripp by telephone regarding the question he asked in
his letter of May 21. I told him that the provision
by the Democratic County Central Committee of voter
registration lists to a candidate would probably be
an in-kind contribution, but that the FPPC has no
jurisdiction over Election Code Section 11702. I then
spoke with Floyd Shimomura of the Attorney General's
Office. Floyd called Mr. Tripp and told him that
he interpreted Election Code Section 11702 to prohibit
county central committees from making in-kind contri-
butions to candidates and that the situation described
in his letter would be an in-kind contribution.

CONTRA COSTA DEMOCRATIC PARTY

COUNTY DEMOCRATIC CENTRAL COMMITTEE


MINOT TRIPP                                MAY 21, 1976
Chairman
723 Ocean Avenue
Pt. Richmond, Ca 94801
(415) 798-2412


Fair Political Practices Commission
1015 L Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

        One of the candidates in the Democratic primary
for Assemblyman from the 11th District, Mr. Gregory Cook,
has requested our Committee to make available to him one
of our copies of the precinct lists of registered voters.
Four copies of these lists are provided to us, free of
charge, by the County Clerk. The campaign of Mr. Cook's
opponent, Assemblyman John T. Knox, has indicated that
it has no objection to our providing the lists in question.
However, some of our members are concerned that providing
the lists might constitute an "in kind" contribution, since
Mr. Cook would have to pay the County Clerk the sum of
$132.50 (plus sales tax) to purchase the lists.

        Could you please advise us, at the earliest
possible date, whether provision of the lists should be
treated as an "in kind" contribution and, if so, whether
it would be proper in view of the legal requirement that
County Central Committees not become involved in primary
campaigns?

                                Very truly yours,

                                [signature]
MWT:ABR                         Minot W. Tripp
